page: Microsoft Azure F48s_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FSv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. This configuration provides 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache per core, and 32 KB L1 data cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-optimized ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 393 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance disk options. It does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on the host CPU for processing tasks. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks place the F48s_v2 in the average range for most compute and memory tasks. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is recorded at 43729.45, while the single-core performance reaches 1444.49. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong read-only small block cached speed of 2495.71 GB/sec, and PassMark memory testing rates the memory subsystem as strong, placing it in the top 25% with a score of 2794.44. However, large block uncached memory operations are slower, with read speeds at 86.68 GB/sec and write speeds at 42.47 GB/sec. Large-scale LLM inference tasks, such as text generation with a 70B model, show weaker performance at 2.72 tokens/sec due to the lack of GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F48s_v2 offers a high density of compute resources relative to memory, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. This resource balance is suitable for batch processing, web serving, and compilation tasks, as demonstrated by its static web serving throughput of 22.87 GB/sec and a Geekbench compilation score of 29309.0. It is also well-suited for medium-scale database operations and network appliances.
description: Microsoft Azure F48s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by Microsoft Hyper-V, it includes 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance is Premium Storage capable and includes 393 GB of local storage, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 43729.45, alongside strong memory subsystem performance in PassMark testing. This server is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as compilation, batch processing, and web serving, where high CPU density is required without massive memory overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48s_v2 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, 96 GB of RAM, and 393 GB of local storage, designed for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48s_v2 is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 393 GB local storage, running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high CPU density, dedicated Intel Xeon cores, and balanced local storage for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
96.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 1:2 core-to-RAM ratio
393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory subsystem performance highlighted by PassMark benchmarks
Suited for CPU-bound tasks, software compilation, and web serving
